texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Course Outcomes: -
texts.text: Course Outcomes: -

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Students  will  be  able  to  understand  basic  principles  &  be  able  to  analyse  various problems of biophysics.
texts.text: Students  will  be  able  to  understand  basic  principles  &  be  able  to  analyse  various problems of biophysics.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Students  will  be  able  to  evaluate  problems  &  results  involving  Acid-base  and  pH chemistry  ,  Atomic  structure  ,.  Solution  &  Dialysis  and  transport  across  biological membrane.
texts.text: Students  will  be  able  to  evaluate  problems  &  results  involving  Acid-base  and  pH chemistry  ,  Atomic  structure  ,.  Solution  &  Dialysis  and  transport  across  biological membrane.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. Students will be able to understand Biochemistry of Carbohydrates , Lipids & Amino Acids and their significance.
texts.text: Students will be able to understand Biochemistry of Carbohydrates , Lipids & Amino Acids and their significance.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Students will be able to understand    clinical & biochemical role of Proteins , Minerals & Vitamins.
texts.text: Students will be able to understand    clinical & biochemical role of Proteins , Minerals & Vitamins.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. Students will be able to evaluate principles of Biochemistry of Enzymes.
texts.text: Students will be able to evaluate principles of Biochemistry of Enzymes.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Suggestive Learning Outcomes:
texts.text: Suggestive Learning Outcomes:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1) Students will be able to understand basic principle of biophysics.
texts.text: Students will be able to understand basic principle of biophysics.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2) Students will be able to analyse various problems related to biophysical principle.
texts.text: Students will be able to analyse various problems related to biophysical principle.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3) Students will be able to evaluate results involving biophysical principles.
texts.text: Students will be able to evaluate results involving biophysical principles.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4) Students will be able to understand basic principle of acid-base chemistry.
texts.text: Students will be able to understand basic principle of acid-base chemistry.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5) Students will be able to analyse various problems related to pH.
texts.text: Students will be able to analyse various problems related to pH.

texts.label: list_item

texts.text: Students will be able to evaluate results involving pH.

texts.orig: Detailed content of the unit:-
texts.text: Detailed content of the unit:-

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Osmosis & osmotic pressure , Surface tension , Colloid & colloidal system, Diffusion, viscosity , Endocytosis, phagocytosis and pinocytosis
texts.text: Osmosis & osmotic pressure , Surface tension , Colloid & colloidal system, Diffusion, viscosity , Endocytosis, phagocytosis and pinocytosis

texts.label: list_item

texts.text: Study of Acid, Base, Buffers, pH, and Handerson-Hasselbalch equation. , Significance of pH in our body , Acidosis and alkalosis

texts.text: Module- II: Atomic structure, Solutions & Dialysis and membrane transport.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Number of class hours: 04

texts.orig: Suggestive Learning Outcomes:
texts.text: Suggestive Learning Outcomes:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1) Students will be able to know application related to isotope.
texts.text: Students will be able to know application related to isotope.

texts.label: list_item

texts.text: Students will be able to analyse various problems related to various types of solutions & will be able to prepare various types of solutions.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3) Students will be able to understand basic principle of dialysis & will be able to understand various types of dialysis.
texts.text: Students will be able to understand basic principle of dialysis & will be able to understand various types of dialysis.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4) Students  will  be  able  to  analyse  various  types  of  transport  across  biological membrane.
texts.text: Students  will  be  able  to  analyse  various  types  of  transport  across  biological membrane.

texts.text: Detailed content of the unit:-

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Isotopes & radio-isotopes , Application of isotopes in medical practice
texts.text: Isotopes & radio-isotopes , Application of isotopes in medical practice

texts.label: list_item

texts.label: list_item

texts.text: Simple diffusion , Active transport , Passive transport , Voltage gated and ligand gated channel.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Module- 1II: Biochemistry of Carbohydrates, Lipids & Amino Acids. Number of class hours: 06
texts.text: Module- 1II: Biochemistry of Carbohydrates, Lipids & Amino Acids. Number of class hours: 06

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Suggestive Learning Outcomes:
texts.text: Suggestive Learning Outcomes:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1) Students will be able to understand biochemical principles of Carbohydrates.
texts.text: Students will be able to understand biochemical principles of Carbohydrates.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2) Students  will  be  able  to  analyse  various  aspects  of  Carbohydrates  &  able  to  determine qualitative and quantitative aspects of Carbohydrates.
texts.text: Students  will  be  able  to  analyse  various  aspects  of  Carbohydrates  &  able  to  determine qualitative and quantitative aspects of Carbohydrates.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3) Students will be able to understand biochemical principles & synthesis of Lipids to analyse clinical role of various lipids.
texts.text: Students will be able to understand biochemical principles & synthesis of Lipids to analyse clinical role of various lipids.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4) Students will be able to understand biochemical principles & clinical role of various amino acids.
texts.text: Students will be able to understand biochemical principles & clinical role of various amino acids.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Detailed content of the unit: -
texts.text: Detailed content of the unit: -

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Definition,  Function  &  Classification  of  Carbohydrates,  Concept  of  Asymmetric Carbon , Empirical formula and structures of Carbohydrates.
texts.text: Definition,  Function  &  Classification  of  Carbohydrates,  Concept  of  Asymmetric Carbon , Empirical formula and structures of Carbohydrates.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Types of Isomerism / Optical Isomerism of monosaccharide with their structures & examples, Properties of Disacchardes & Polysaccharides with their structures, Principles of Qualitative & Semi-quantitative Clinical diagnosis related to Carbohydrate chemistry.
texts.text: Types of Isomerism / Optical Isomerism of monosaccharide with their structures & examples, Properties of Disacchardes & Polysaccharides with their structures, Principles of Qualitative & Semi-quantitative Clinical diagnosis related to Carbohydrate chemistry.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. Definition,  function  &  classification  of  Lipids.  Esterification  &  Synthesis  of  fats. Essential Fatty acids - concepts , structure & IUPAC Nomenclature, PUFA,MUFA.
texts.text: Definition,  function  &  classification  of  Lipids.  Esterification  &  Synthesis  of  fats. Essential Fatty acids - concepts , structure & IUPAC Nomenclature, PUFA,MUFA.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Isomerism  and  properties  of  fatty  acids.  Quantitative  estimation  of  unsaturation through  Iodine  number  ,  Saponification  number.  Clinical  Significance  of  Fats  , Atherosclerosis , CBAG.Clinical significance of HDL, LDL, VLDL, TG etc.
texts.text: Isomerism  and  properties  of  fatty  acids.  Quantitative  estimation  of  unsaturation through  Iodine  number  ,  Saponification  number.  Clinical  Significance  of  Fats  , Atherosclerosis , CBAG.Clinical significance of HDL, LDL, VLDL, TG etc.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. Amino  acids  &  their  structures,  classification,  isomerism,  properties.  Concept  of Zwitter ion & effect of pH on charge , Iso-electic pH.
texts.text: Amino  acids  &  their  structures,  classification,  isomerism,  properties.  Concept  of Zwitter ion & effect of pH on charge , Iso-electic pH.

texts.text: Clinical significance of proteins, Proteinuria & related clinical concepts. Physiological aspects of functional & structural proteins.

texts.label: list_item

texts.label: list_item

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 7. Role of Electrolytes, Macro & Micro elements.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Suggestive Learning Outcomes:
texts.text: Suggestive Learning Outcomes:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1) Students will be able to understand biochemical properties of Enzymes.
texts.text: Students will be able to understand biochemical properties of Enzymes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2) Students will be able to enumerate details of factors effecting enzyme activity.
texts.text: Students will be able to enumerate details of factors effecting enzyme activity.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3) Students will be able to understand concepts of turnover number & able to analyse various enzyme inhibitions.
texts.text: Students will be able to understand concepts of turnover number & able to analyse various enzyme inhibitions.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Detailed content of the unit:-
texts.text: Detailed content of the unit:-

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Definition and classification of enzymes.
texts.text: Definition and classification of enzymes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Kinetics of enzyme, Concepts of Km & Vmax.
texts.text: Kinetics of enzyme, Concepts of Km & Vmax.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. Mechanism of action & Concept of activation energy and enzyme substrate complex , Models of enzyme action , Concepts of active site , allosterism.
texts.text: Mechanism of action & Concept of activation energy and enzyme substrate complex , Models of enzyme action , Concepts of active site , allosterism.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Factors affecting enzyme activity & graphical representation.
texts.text: Factors affecting enzyme activity & graphical representation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. Concepts of co-enzyme, co-factor & prosthetic groups.
texts.text: Concepts of co-enzyme, co-factor & prosthetic groups.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6. Mechalis Menten Equation, Reciprocal curve.
texts.text: Mechalis Menten Equation, Reciprocal curve.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 7. Enzyme inhibition - Competitive, non-competitive & un-competitive , Feed back inhibition , Introduction to drug design.
texts.text: Enzyme inhibition - Competitive, non-competitive & un-competitive , Feed back inhibition , Introduction to drug design.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 8. Clinical importance of Enzymes & Isozymes in Diagnostics & Therapeutics.
texts.text: Clinical importance of Enzymes & Isozymes in Diagnostics & Therapeutics.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Course Content:-
texts.text: Course Content:-

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Internships are educational and career development  opportunities, providing practical experience in a field or discipline. The Summer Internship-I is a student centric activity that would expose Technical students to the industrial environment, which cannot be simulated in the  classroom  and  hence  creating  competent  professionals  for  the  industry.  They  are structured,  short-term,  supervised  placements  often  focused  around  particular  tasks  or projects  with  defined  timescales.  An  internship  may  be  compensated,  non-compensated  or some time may be paid. The internship has to be meaningful and mutually beneficial to the intern  and  the  organization.  It  is  important  that  the  objectives  and  the  activities  of  the internship program are clearly defined and understood. Following are the intended objectives of internship training:
texts.text: Internships are educational and career development  opportunities, providing practical experience in a field or discipline. The Summer Internship-I is a student centric activity that would expose Technical students to the industrial environment, which cannot be simulated in the  classroom  and  hence  creating  competent  professionals  for  the  industry.  They  are structured,  short-term,  supervised  placements  often  focused  around  particular  tasks  or projects  with  defined  timescales.  An  internship  may  be  compensated,  non-compensated  or some time may be paid. The internship has to be meaningful and mutually beneficial to the intern  and  the  organization.  It  is  important  that  the  objectives  and  the  activities  of  the internship program are clearly defined and understood. Following are the intended objectives of internship training:

texts.text: Will  expose  Technical  students  to  the  industrial  environment,  which  cannot  be simulated  in  the  classroom  and  hence  creating  competent  professionals  for  the industry.

texts.label: list_item

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: STUDENT'S DIARY/ DAILY LOG
texts.text: STUDENT'S DIARY/ DAILY LOG

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The main purpose of writing daily diary is to cultivate the habit of documenting and to encourage the students to search for details. It develops the students' thought process and reasoning  abilities.  The  students  should  record  in  the  daily  training  diary  the  day-to-day account of the observations, impressions, information gathered and suggestions given, if any. It should contain the sketches & drawings related to the observations made by the students.
texts.text: The main purpose of writing daily diary is to cultivate the habit of documenting and to encourage the students to search for details. It develops the students' thought process and reasoning  abilities.  The  students  should  record  in  the  daily  training  diary  the  day-to-day account of the observations, impressions, information gathered and suggestions given, if any. It should contain the sketches & drawings related to the observations made by the students.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The daily training diary should be signed at the end of each dayby the supervisor/ in charge of the section where the student has been working. The diary should also be shown to the Faculty Mentor visiting the industry from time to time and get ratified on the day of his visit.
texts.text: The daily training diary should be signed at the end of each dayby the supervisor/ in charge of the section where the student has been working. The diary should also be shown to the Faculty Mentor visiting the industry from time to time and get ratified on the day of his visit.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Student's Diary and Internship Report should be submitted by the students along with attendance record  and  an  evaluation  sheet  duly  signed  and  stamped  by  the  industry  to  the Institute immediately after the completion of the training. It will be evaluated on the basis of the following criteria:

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: INTERNSHIP REPORT
texts.text: INTERNSHIP REPORT

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After completion of Internship, the student should prepare a comprehensive report to indicate  what  he  has  observed  and  learnt  in  the  training  period.  The  student  may  contact Industrial  Supervisor/  Faculty  Mentor/TPO  for  assigning  special  topics  and  problems  and should prepare the final report on the assigned topics. Daily diary will also help to a great extent  in  writing  the  industrial  report  since  much  of  the  information  has  already  been incorporated by the student into the daily diary. The training report should be signed by the Internship Supervisor, TPO and Faculty Mentor. The Internship report will be evaluated on the basis of following criteria:
texts.text: After completion of Internship, the student should prepare a comprehensive report to indicate  what  he  has  observed  and  learnt  in  the  training  period.  The  student  may  contact Industrial  Supervisor/  Faculty  Mentor/TPO  for  assigning  special  topics  and  problems  and should prepare the final report on the assigned topics. Daily diary will also help to a great extent  in  writing  the  industrial  report  since  much  of  the  information  has  already  been incorporated by the student into the daily diary. The training report should be signed by the Internship Supervisor, TPO and Faculty Mentor. The Internship report will be evaluated on the basis of following criteria:

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Course Content:-
texts.text: Course Content:-

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The minor project topic should be selected / chosen to ensure the satisfaction of the urgent need to establish a direct link between education, national development and productivity and thus reduce the gap between the world of work and the world of study. The course should have the following-
texts.text: The minor project topic should be selected / chosen to ensure the satisfaction of the urgent need to establish a direct link between education, national development and productivity and thus reduce the gap between the world of work and the world of study. The course should have the following-

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1) Perform detailed study about various components of a project.
texts.text: Perform detailed study about various components of a project.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2) Study about methodologies and professional way of documentation and communication related to project work.
texts.text: Study about methodologies and professional way of documentation and communication related to project work.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3) Develop idea about problem formulation.
texts.text: Develop idea about problem formulation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4) Knowledge of how to organize, scope, plan, do and act within a project thesis.
texts.text: Knowledge of how to organize, scope, plan, do and act within a project thesis.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5) Familiarity with specific tools (i.e. hardware equipment and software) relevant to the project selected.
texts.text: Familiarity with specific tools (i.e. hardware equipment and software) relevant to the project selected.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6) Demonstrate the implementation of a minor project work.
texts.text: Demonstrate the implementation of a minor project work.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Internships  may  be  full-time  or  part-time;  they  are  full-time  in  the  summer  vacation  and part-time during the academic session.
texts.text: Internships  may  be  full-time  or  part-time;  they  are  full-time  in  the  summer  vacation  and part-time during the academic session.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: (## During the summer vacation after 4 th Semester, students are ready for industrial experience. Therefore,  they  may  choose  to  undergo  Internship  /Innovation  /Entrepreneurship  related activities.  Students  may  choose  either  to  work  on  innovation  or  entrepreneurial  activities resulting in start-up or undergo internship with industry/ NGO's/ Government organizations/ Micro/  Small/  Medium  enterprises  to  make  themselves  ready  for  the  industry.  In  case  a student  want  to  pursue  his/herfamily  business  and  don't  want  to  undergo  internship,  a declaration by a parent may be submitted directly to the TPO.)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Course Content:-
texts.text: Course Content:-

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Internships are educational and career development  opportunities, providing practical experience in a field or discipline. The Summer Internship-II is a student centric activity that would expose Technical students to the industrial environment, which cannot be simulated in the  classroom  and  hence  creating  competent  professionals  for  the  industry.  They  are structured,  short-term,  supervised  placements  often  focused  around  particular  tasks  or projects  with  defined  timescales.  An  internship  may  be  compensated,  non-compensated  or some time may be paid. The internship has to be meaningful and mutually beneficial to the intern  and  the  organization.  It  is  important  that  the  objectives  and  the  activities  of  the internship program are clearly defined and understood. Following are the intended objectives of internship training:
texts.text: Internships are educational and career development  opportunities, providing practical experience in a field or discipline. The Summer Internship-II is a student centric activity that would expose Technical students to the industrial environment, which cannot be simulated in the  classroom  and  hence  creating  competent  professionals  for  the  industry.  They  are structured,  short-term,  supervised  placements  often  focused  around  particular  tasks  or projects  with  defined  timescales.  An  internship  may  be  compensated,  non-compensated  or some time may be paid. The internship has to be meaningful and mutually beneficial to the intern  and  the  organization.  It  is  important  that  the  objectives  and  the  activities  of  the internship program are clearly defined and understood. Following are the intended objectives of internship training:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Will expose Technical students to the industrial environment, which cannot be simulated in the classroom and hence creating competent professionals for the industry.
texts.text: Will expose Technical students to the industrial environment, which cannot be simulated in the classroom and hence creating competent professionals for the industry.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Provide  possible  opportunities  to  learn,  understand  and  sharpen  the  real  time  technical  / managerial skills required at the job.
texts.text: Provide  possible  opportunities  to  learn,  understand  and  sharpen  the  real  time  technical  / managerial skills required at the job.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. Exposure to the current technological developments relevant to the subject area of training.
texts.text: Exposure to the current technological developments relevant to the subject area of training.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Experience  gained  from  the  'Industrial  Internship'  in  classroom  will  be  used  in  classroom discussions.
texts.text: Experience  gained  from  the  'Industrial  Internship'  in  classroom  will  be  used  in  classroom discussions.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. Create conditions conducive to quest for knowledge and its applicability on the job.
texts.text: Create conditions conducive to quest for knowledge and its applicability on the job.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6. Learn to apply the Technical knowledge in real industrial situations.
texts.text: Learn to apply the Technical knowledge in real industrial situations.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 7. Gain experience in writing Technical reports/projects.
texts.text: Gain experience in writing Technical reports/projects.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 8. Expose students to the engineer's responsibilities and ethics.
texts.text: Expose students to the engineer's responsibilities and ethics.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 9. Familiarize  with  various  materials,  processes,  products  and  their  applications  along  with relevant aspects of quality control.
texts.text: Familiarize  with  various  materials,  processes,  products  and  their  applications  along  with relevant aspects of quality control.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 10. Promote academic, professional and/or personal development.
texts.text: Promote academic, professional and/or personal development.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 11. Expose the students to future employers.
texts.text: Expose the students to future employers.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 12. Understand the social, economic and administrative considerations that influence the working environment of industrial organizations
texts.text: Understand the social, economic and administrative considerations that influence the working environment of industrial organizations

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 13. Understand the psychology of the workers and their habits, attitudes and approach to problem solving.
texts.text: Understand the psychology of the workers and their habits, attitudes and approach to problem solving.

texts.orig: STUDENT'S DIARY/ DAILY LOG
texts.text: STUDENT'S DIARY/ DAILY LOG

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The main purpose of writing daily diary is to cultivate the habit of documenting and to encourage the students to search for details. It develops the students' thought process and reasoning  abilities.  The  students  should  record  in  the  daily  training  diary  the  day-to-day account of the observations, impressions, information gathered and suggestions given, if any. It should contain the sketches & drawings related to the observations made by the students.
texts.text: The main purpose of writing daily diary is to cultivate the habit of documenting and to encourage the students to search for details. It develops the students' thought process and reasoning  abilities.  The  students  should  record  in  the  daily  training  diary  the  day-to-day account of the observations, impressions, information gathered and suggestions given, if any. It should contain the sketches & drawings related to the observations made by the students.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The daily training diary should be signed at the end of each day by the supervisor/ in charge of the section where the student has been working. The diary should also be shown to the Faculty Mentor visiting the industry from time to time and get ratified on the day of his visit.
texts.text: The daily training diary should be signed at the end of each day by the supervisor/ in charge of the section where the student has been working. The diary should also be shown to the Faculty Mentor visiting the industry from time to time and get ratified on the day of his visit.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: INTERNSHIP REPORT
texts.text: INTERNSHIP REPORT

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After completion of Internship, the student should prepare a comprehensive report to indicate  what  he  has  observed  and  learnt  in  the  training  period.  The  student  may  contact Industrial  Supervisor/  Faculty  Mentor/TPO  for  assigning  special  topics  and  problems  and should prepare the final report on the assigned topics. Daily diary will also help to a great extent  in  writing  the  industrial  report  since  much  of  the  information  has  already  been incorporated by the student into the daily diary. The training report should be signed by the Internship Supervisor, TPO and Faculty Mentor. The Internship report will be evaluated on the basis of following criteria:
texts.text: After completion of Internship, the student should prepare a comprehensive report to indicate  what  he  has  observed  and  learnt  in  the  training  period. Daily diary will also help to a great extent  in  writing  the  industrial  report  since  much  of  the  information  has  already  been incorporated by the student into the daily diary. texts.orig: After completion of the course, students will be able to:
texts.text: After completion of the course, students will be able to:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: C.O. 1: Demonstrate a sound technical knowledge of their selected project topic and the knowledge, skills and attitudes of a professional engineer (K2).
texts.text: C.O. 1: Demonstrate a sound technical knowledge of their selected project topic and the knowledge, skills and attitudes of a professional engineer (K2).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: C.O. 2: Develop the skill of working in a Team (K3).
texts.text: C.O. 2: Develop the skill of working in a Team (K3).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: C.O. 3: Design engineering solutions to complex problems utilising a systems approach (K6).
texts.text: C.O. 3: Design engineering solutions to complex problems utilising a systems approach (K6).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: C.O. 4: Design the solution of an engineering project involving latest tools and techniques (K6).
texts.text: C.O. 4: Design the solution of an engineering project involving latest tools and techniques (K6).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: C.O. 5: Develop the skill of effective communication with engineers and the community at large in written an oral forms (K3).
texts.text: C.O. 5: Develop the skill of effective communication with engineers and the community at large in written an oral forms (K3).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Course Content:-
texts.text: Course Content:-

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The major project topic should be selected / chosen to ensure the satisfaction of the urgent need to establish a direct link between education, national development and productivity and thus reduce the gap between the world of work and the world of study. The course should provide the scope to develop the following by the students-
texts.text: The major project topic should be selected / chosen to ensure the satisfaction of the urgent need to establish a direct link between education, national development and productivity and thus reduce the gap between the world of work and the world of study. The course should provide the scope to develop the following by the students-

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1) Develop sound knowledge about the domain of the project work.
texts.text: Develop sound knowledge about the domain of the project work.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2) Perform detailed study about various components of a project.
texts.text: Perform detailed study about various components of a project.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3) Learn to be an important member of a team for successful execution of a project work.
texts.text: Learn to be an important member of a team for successful execution of a project work.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4) Study about methodologies and professional way of documentation and communication related to project work.
texts.text: Study about methodologies and professional way of documentation and communication related to project work.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5) Develop idea about problem formulation, finding the solution of a complex engineering problem.
texts.text: Develop idea about problem formulation, finding the solution of a complex engineering problem.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6) Develop project report as per the suggested format to communicate the findings of the project work.
texts.text: Develop project report as per the suggested format to communicate the findings of the project work.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 7) Acquire the skill of effective oral communication to the fellow engineers and people in the society at large.
texts.text: Acquire the skill of effective oral communication to the fellow engineers and people in the society at large.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 8) Knowledge of how to organize, scope, plan, do and act within a project thesis.
texts.text: Knowledge of how to organize, scope, plan, do and act within a project thesis.

texts.label: list_item

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Course Outcome:-
texts.text: Course Outcome:-

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After completion of the course, students will be able to:
texts.text: After completion of the course, students will be able to:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: C.O.1: Demonstrate a thorough and systematic understanding of a seminar topic (K2).
texts.text: C.O.1: Demonstrate a thorough and systematic understanding of a seminar topic (K2).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: C.O. 2: Identify the methodologies and professional way of documentation and communication (K3).
texts.text: C.O. 2: Identify the methodologies and professional way of documentation and communication (K3).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: C.O.3: Demonstrate the ability to construct a report consistent with expectations of the topic, including an appropriate organization, style, voice, and tone (K3).
texts.text: C.O.3: Demonstrate the ability to construct a report consistent with expectations of the topic, including an appropriate organization, style, voice, and tone (K3).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: C.O.4: Develop the ability to follow discussions, oral arguments, and presentations, noting main  points  or  evidence  and  tracking  through  different  comments  given  by  the  audience (K3).
texts.text: C.O.4: Develop the ability to follow discussions, oral arguments, and presentations, noting main  points  or  evidence  and  tracking  through  different  comments  given  by  the  audience (K3).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: C.O.5: Develop the communication skill as a speaker (K3).
texts.text: C.O.5: Develop the communication skill as a speaker (K3).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Course Content:-
texts.text: Course Content:-

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The seminar topics may be any aspect of the science and technology, entrepreneurship or any contemporary social issues to be solved by specific branch of engineering and technology (For example, Water logging problems in a particular city may be a seminar topic for Civil Engineering Students) must be approved by the instructor in advance.
texts.text: The seminar topics may be any aspect of the science and technology, entrepreneurship or any contemporary social issues to be solved by specific branch of engineering and technology (For example, Water logging problems in a particular city may be a seminar topic for Civil Engineering Students) must be approved by the instructor in advance.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The course should have the following-
texts.text: The course should have the following-

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1) Practice speaking in front of a scientific audience.
texts.text: Practice speaking in front of a scientific audience.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2) Explore topics in detail.
texts.text: Explore topics in detail.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3) Research topics and organize presentations.
texts.text: Research topics and organize presentations.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4) To improve as speakers, each student will receive feedback from the fellow students and the instructor.
texts.text: To improve as speakers, each student will receive feedback from the fellow students and the instructor.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5) PowerPoint, Key Note or overheads are acceptable media for Visual aids. Visual aids should look professional and be readable in the entire room; use spell check and proofread for typographical errors.
texts.text: PowerPoint, Key Note or overheads are acceptable media for Visual aids. Visual aids should look professional and be readable in the entire room; use spell check and proofread for typographical errors.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6) Students have to submit a hard copy contains detailed outline (4-5 pages) of their presentation and also a brief abstract (one or two paragraphs; 250 words max.) describing their presentation.
texts.text: Students have to submit a hard copy contains detailed outline (4-5 pages) of their presentation and also a brief abstract (one or two paragraphs; 250 words max.) describing their presentation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 7) Each student will give 20-minute presentations followed by 3 minutes of question-answer session.
texts.text: Each student will give 20-minute presentations followed by 3 minutes of question-answer session.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Proposal Seminar Format for Students:
texts.text: Proposal Seminar Format for Students:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Introduce yourself.
texts.text:  Introduce yourself.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Give an introduction and background information on your topic.    What relevant research has been performed previously?
texts.text:  Give an introduction and background information on your topic.    What relevant research has been performed previously?

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  State the problem(s) that remain unanswered.
texts.text:  State the problem(s) that remain unanswered.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Clearly state your objectives and give the specific hypotheses you wish to test.
texts.text:  Clearly state your objectives and give the specific hypotheses you wish to test.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Describe the methodology you will use to test your hypotheses.    Be sure you fully understand your chosen methods.    Give reasons why you chose these methods over other approaches.
texts.text:  Describe the methodology you will use to test your hypotheses.    Be sure you fully understand your chosen methods.    Give reasons why you chose these methods over other approaches.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Present any data you have collected thus far.
texts.text:  Present any data you have collected thus far.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Describe what remains to be done, and what you expect to find.
texts.text:  Describe what remains to be done, and what you expect to find.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Explain the significance of your findings (or potential future findings).
texts.text: Explain the significance of your findings (or potential future findings).
